purpose: Route x capability map for the CAD OS Layer. References (does NOT modify) config/autocad_router_capabilities.json (schema ariadne.autocad_router_capabilities.v2, 11 routes). v1 capabilities file remains the live-probe source of truth for route availability; this file adds the operation-family -> route capability projection that operations.v2.json consumes.

references.note: Route availability, requires[], fallback_to[], and side_effects are owned by config/autocad_router_capabilities.json and regenerated by tools/probe_routes.py on every -Action status/select/run. This file MUST NOT be trusted for live availability; consult the v1 probe sidecar reports/autocad_router_status_latest.json instead.
references.vocabulary_note: The 'hosts' arrays in routes below use the physical host names {objectdbx, core_console, native_arx, managed, full_autocad} from host_matrix.v2.json. operations.v2.json (bound to operation_registry.v2.schema.json) uses the 4-value execution_host_class enum {dbx, coreconsole, arx_adapter, full_autocad}; see host_matrix.v2.json registry_vocabulary_crosswalk to translate. operation_families[] below reference operations.v2.json ids verbatim.

routes._comment: 11 routes from v1 capabilities. capabilities[] = operation-family capabilities this route can satisfy. operation_families[] = operations.v2 ids routed here. native_lane shows which ARIADNE_* command (if any) the route drives. available_truth_source points back to the v1 probe.

dwg_truth_autocad.notes: Authoritative DWG control plane. Hosts the 29 wired ops + the v2 target families. The 1 managed op (inspect.database.summary) runs via ARIADNE_CAD_JOB; the other 28 via ARIADNE_NATIVE_JOB. geometry_extract_truth is the read/truth lane (separate dwg_geometry_extract.v1 schema).

dxf_fast_secondary.cross_engine_role: Secondary read/cross-check lane for DXF without AutoCAD (375=375 cross-engine parity vs dwg_truth_autocad). Read-only; never an authoritative write lane.
dxf_fast_secondary.notes: python:dxf_fast_secondary handler for graph/entity reads when no AutoCAD host is available. Lossy vs native truth (no dynamic-block/xdata fidelity guarantees).

dwg_libredwg_sidecar.cross_engine_role: GPL sidecar cross-check ONLY. Separate process; never link/import into the production router.
dwg_libredwg_sidecar.notes: DWG read without AutoCAD for crosscheck/diff. Sidecar-only per GPL boundary.

solid_brep_occ.notes: STEP/BREP/IGES topology lane. Not an AutoCAD DWG operation host; complements the catalog brep_solids family for non-DWG solids.

parametric_rebuild.notes: Generation-only (output STEP/STL/SVG). Not a DWG read/write op host.

ifc_bim_semantic.notes: IFC/BIM semantic read. Out of the AutoCAD op surface; referenced for cross-domain BIM alignment only.

geo_vector_route.notes: SHP/GeoJSON/DGN vector IO + CRS. Non-DWG (DXF/DGN secondary read only).

pdf_svg_vector_route.notes: PDF/SVG vector path extraction. Candidate downstream for a future headless render.layout overlay (render itself remains blocked on the plot host).

raster_compare_route.notes: PNG/JPG render SSIM compare. Candidate visual-QA backend for diff.before_after when a rendered image pair exists.

capability_to_routes._comment: Reverse index: capability -> routes that satisfy it (priority order). Router picks the cheapest available host/route via the v1 fallback_to chain.

invariants.availability_truth: A route is available ONLY if every REQUIRED tool imports/resolves (live probe). This file never asserts availability; it only maps capabilities. Consult reports/autocad_router_status_latest.json.
invariants.no_modify_v1: config/autocad_router_capabilities.json is READ-ONLY to this lane and is not modified.
invariants.no_fake_success: Unavailable/unimplemented capability => explicit not_implemented/unavailable/blocked, never a faked pass.
